name: Zomato-Style Restaurant Search V2 — Streams Edition
scenario: Remember the Zomato Restaurant Search project from Topic 6.5? It used loops and manual filtering. Now rewrite every query using Stream API — no more for-loops. The same data, dramatically cleaner code. This refactoring from Module 6 collections-style to Module 8 streams-style is exactly what happens when teams upgrade their codebase.
description: Rewrite all restaurant queries using Stream API: filter by cuisine/rating/price/city, sort, paginate, compute statistics, find best options — all as stream pipelines. Zero manual loops allowed.

core.description: `Restaurant` class: name, city, cuisine, pricePerPerson (double), rating (double). Getters + toString.
`RestaurantSearchV2.searchByCuisine(List<Restaurant>, String)` — stream pipeline: filter cuisine (case-insensitive), sort by rating desc, return List<Restaurant>.
`affordableHighRated(List<Restaurant>, double maxPrice, double minRating)` — stream: filter both conditions, sort by rating desc.
`cityStats(List<Restaurant>, String city)` — stream: filter by city, compute avg rating (mapToDouble.average), count, find most expensive (max).
`topRestaurants(List<Restaurant>, int n)` — stream: sort by rating desc, limit n, collect.
`uniqueCuisines(List<Restaurant>)` — stream: map cuisine, distinct, sorted, collect.
`recommendationSummary(List<Restaurant>)` — stream pipeline producing a String: names of top 3 by rating, joined by ' > '.
main() uses ALL methods above. Zero for-loops allowed in the stream methods — stream pipelines only.
Use at least 3 method references in the solution.

bonus.description: Add `getPage(List<Restaurant>, int page, int size, Comparator<Restaurant> sort)` using sorted + skip + limit — generic pagination.
Add `anyStarRestaurant(List<Restaurant>, double minRating)` — stream anyMatch: is there any restaurant rated >= minRating?

data_to_use:
Use the same data as Topic 6.5 restaurant project:
- Spice Garden, Hyderabad, Indian, ₹350, 4.3
- Noodle House, Hyderabad, Chinese, ₹280, 4.1
- Pizza Place, Mumbai, Italian, ₹500, 4.5
- Curry Leaf, Mumbai, Indian, ₹400, 4.7
- Wok & Roll, Hyderabad, Chinese, ₹320, 4.2
- Biryani Bros, Hyderabad, Indian, ₹300, 4.4
- Pasta Corner, Mumbai, Italian, ₹550, 4.0
- Dim Sum Delight, Mumbai, Chinese, ₹450, 4.6
example_output:
=== Indian restaurants (by rating) ===
Curry Leaf: 4.7
Biryani Bros: 4.4
Spice Garden: 4.3

=== Affordable & highly rated (≤₹350, ≥4.0) ===
Biryani Bros: 4.4 ₹300
Spice Garden: 4.3 ₹350
Wok & Roll: 4.2 ₹320
Noodle House: 4.1 ₹280

=== Hyderabad stats ===
Count: 4 | Avg rating: 4.25 | Priciest: Spice Garden

=== Top 3 restaurants ===
Curry Leaf, Pizza Place, Dim Sum Delight

=== Unique cuisines ===
[Chinese, Indian, Italian]

=== Recommendation summary ===
Top 3: Curry Leaf > Dim Sum Delight > Pizza Place

criteria: All 6 query methods use streams (30 pts)
Zero for-loops in stream methods (10 pts)
Results match expected for all queries (25 pts)
At least 3 method references used (10 pts)
Comment showing how streams simplified the code vs loops (10 pts)

ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. The core requirement: every method body is a stream pipeline with NO for-loops. cityStats must compute average via mapToDouble.average() not a manual sum. recommendationSummary uses Collectors.joining(' > '). Method references: use at least Comparator.comparingDouble() with method reference, and map(Restaurant::getName) etc.
submission_instructions: Save as ZomatoSearchV2.java. Add a comment at the top: '// Refactored from Topic 6.5 (loops) to Topic 8.3 (streams)'. Push to GitHub.
tips_for_success: cityStats: filter by city, use .mapToDouble(Restaurant::getRating).average().orElse(0.0) for avg
recommendationSummary: sort desc, limit(3), map name, Collectors.joining(' > ')
